You are a strict reviewer checking a scientific paper against its guardrails and numerical results.

GUARDRAILS:
{guardrails}

NUMERICAL RESULTS (from results.json):
Training turn accuracy: {train_ta}
Test turn accuracy: {test_ta}

Civilization metrics:
{civ_metrics}

Check the article for:
1. PROHIBITED claims only: "predicts", "demonstrates", "proves", "solves", "validated" (without qualification), causal claims ("caused", "triggered", "responded to"), overclaiming ("solves the Fermi Paradox", "early warning system"), normative claims
2. REQUIRED CAVEATS — all 8 must appear somewhere
3. TONE violations — claims at wrong confidence level
4. CONSISTENCY — article numbers must match results.json numbers

DO NOT flag: "propose", "argue", "suggest", "hypothesize", "identifies", "is consistent with", "aligns with", "our results indicate" — these are permitted academic language.

Respond with JSON only:
{{
    "violations": [
        {{
            "type": "prohibited_claim | missing_caveat | tone | inconsistent_number",
            "severity": "critical | minor",
            "quote": "exact quote from article",
            "fix": "how to fix"
        }}
    ],
    "verdict": "pass | fail",
    "summary": "one-sentence assessment"
}}

CRITICAL: temperature=0, be deterministic. Any prohibited claim = fail. Any missing caveat = fail. Any number mismatch = fail.
